title: Pod sage-attention not installing â†’ SDPA fallback â†’ slower LTX sampling. Fix the runtime sage bootstrap (Blackwell/Ada arch-list).
description:
PERF. The product Pod's sage-attention runtime bootstrap (start.sh: pip install sageattention --target=/workspace/cubric/pylibs onto the volume on first boot) PRODUCES NOTHING on the live Pods tested this session: `.sage_arch` stamp empty, `import sageattention` -> ModuleNotFoundError, and the running ComfyUI cmdline has NO `--use-sage-attention`. So every remote LTX gen ran on PyTorch SDPA (the fallback), not sage. Sage is ~2-3x faster than SDPA for diffusion â†’ this is a real sampling speedup left on the table. Confirmed on BOTH a 5090 (Blackwell sm_120) and a 4090 (Ada sm_89) this session.

SUSPECTED CAUSE (from docs/gotchas.md:185, the Builder image learned this): the runtime `pip install sageattention --no-build-isolation` compiles CUDA kernels, but on a GPU-less or arch-mismatched build it 'silently falls back to SDPA'. The Builder image SOURCE-BUILDS sage with `TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST="8.6;8.9;12.0"` (Ada=8.9, Blackwell=12.0) â€” the product Pod's start.sh bootstrap likely lacks the arch list / build deps, so the compile fails and start.sh swallows it as 'non-fatal'. A bare `pip install sageattention` on the live Pod also produced nothing (user ran it).

ALSO: the user noted past sage issues â€” UNSURE if Blackwell-only or both. gotchas.md:147-149 documents sage as a 2x SLOWDOWN on WINDOWS (Triton JIT fails on embedded python) â€” but that is LOCAL/Windows-only; Linux Pods are 'unaffected' per that note. So on the Pod, sage SHOULD help â€” the problem is it never installs.

TASKS:
1. Reproduce: why does the start.sh sage bootstrap produce an empty pylibs? Check start.sh:74-101 (the bootstrap block) â€” does it set TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST? Does the compile error get logged or swallowed?
2. Port the Builder's working pattern: SOURCE-BUILD sage with the arch list (8.6;8.9;12.0) so it compiles for Ada+Blackwell. Possibly bake into the image instead of runtime-installing (the runtime ~5-15min compile is also slow + fragile).
3. Verify on a live Pod: `.sage_arch` populated, `import sageattention` OK, ComfyUI cmdline shows `--use-sage-attention`, and sampling is measurably faster.
4. Consider per-arch: if Blackwell sage is problematic, allow SDPA on cu128 but sage on cu124 (or vice versa) â€” settle from test data.

NEEDS AN IMAGE REBUILD (batch with the v0.9.0 cycle: MPI-139 v0.26.0 + MPI-146 per-card VRAM). BRANCH: RunPod / mpi-ci only. Image build scripts live in c:\AI\Mpi\mpi-ci\cubric-vision-pod\ (start.sh sage block) + cubric-vision-builder\install_nodes.sh (the working source-build reference). || MERGED-IN (MPI-146, 2026-06-28): per-card VRAM mode also ships in this same v0.10.0 image. start.sh exports CUBRIC_VRAM_MODE (>=28GB -> --normalvram, else --lowvram; threshold is 28 not 32 because a 5090 reports 31GiB usable and a 4090 reports 23 -> 28 cleanly splits them); wrapper _build_cmd uses self.vram_mode instead of a hardcoded --lowvram. Co-ships + co-verifies with sage in one rebuild. LIVE BUG still open: the normalvram path has never run clean on a Pod (the old >=32 threshold forced lowvram on the 5090 -> RAM OOM hang); needs the >=28 fix + a fresh cu128 retest. Sister card MPI-155 mirrors this on the LOCAL engine (routes/comfy.js) - do this Pod side first, 155 copies the threshold.
activeSessionTitle: Bake sage per-arch + per-card VRAM into product Pod (v0.10.0 batch)
